what_it_does: This file provides a Vitest globalSetup module that sends one throwaway HTTP POST to a served embedding endpoint before the test suite runs, in order to absorb the endpoint's cold-model-load latency outside any timed test. If the endpoint is unreachable or returns an error, it logs a warning and returns without failing the suite.
observations: Single exported async function `setup` with no teardown export.
POST body sends `input: ['recollection test suite warmup']`.
Uses `AbortController` with a 60-second timeout to cover a ~16s observed cold load.
Fail-soft behavior: non-OK responses and fetch exceptions are logged as warnings and swallowed.
Comment explains the rationale: cold start is non-deterministic and would otherwise land in the first embedding test/hook.
standouts: Explicit warmup to move embedding endpoint cold-start latency out of individual test timeouts.
Fail-soft setup: does not gate the suite when the embed endpoint is absent, letting non-embedding tests run and embedding tests fail with their own diagnostics.
conventions: Follows Vitest `globalSetup` convention by exporting an async `setup` function.
Uses environment-variable override for service URL.
concerns: No exported `teardown` to clean up any state created by the warmup request.
A 60-second unreachable endpoint can delay suite startup by a full minute before giving up.
No retry logic; a transient blip during warmup still logs a warning and proceeds.
The warmup request body is arbitrary and may not match the shape expected by all embedding endpoint implementations.
notes: A fail-soft Vitest global setup that pays the embedding endpoint cold-start cost once so individual tests see warm response times.
questions: How and where is the embedding endpoint started before this globalSetup runs?
Is there a matching `teardown` exported elsewhere, or is none needed?
Which tests are 'embedding-dependent' and how do they report their own failure?
What is the exact schema/expected body for the endpoint at `v1/embeddings`?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (warm the embedding endpoint once, fail-soft if unreachable) matches the implementation.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make embedding-related tests deterministic and timeout-stable by front-loading the endpoint's one-time model-load latency, while keeping the rest of the suite runnable even when the embed service is not available.
novelty: It treats the embedding endpoint's cold-model-load as a test-infrastructure timing hazard and explicitly absorbs it in global setup with a long timeout and graceful failure, rather than letting the first test pay the cost.
contribution: Provides a single, isolated warmup call that moves embedding endpoint cold-start latency outside per-test timeouts, and contributes a fail-soft policy that preserves suite execution when the endpoint is absent.
summary_for_embedding: A Vitest globalSetup module that sends one warmup request to a local embedding HTTP endpoint before the suite runs, with a 60-second abort timeout and warning-only error handling.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to make embedding-dependent tests deterministic by absorbing the endpoint's cold-model-load delay in setup rather than inside the first timed test or hook.

symbols.does: Sends a single warmup HTTP POST to the configured embedding endpoint before the test suite runs.
symbols.description: The Vitest globalSetup `setup` function. It fetches `EMBED_URL` (default `http://127.0.0.1:8007/v1/embeddings`) with a 60-second abortable request, sending `input: ['recollection test suite warmup']`. It logs warnings for non-OK responses or unreachable endpoints and swallows exceptions so the suite continues.
